prose:
I have calculated the Fukui reactivity indices for furan using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier 'standard') in the gas phase. The calculation was performed on the geometry provided in `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz` with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1.

The Fukui indices predict sites for nucleophilic (f+), electrophilic (f-), and radical (f0) attack:

| Atom | Symbol | f+      | f-      | f0      |
|------|--------|---------|---------|---------|
| 1    | O      | -1.3316 | 0.0838  | -0.6239 |
| 2    | C      | 1.2287  | 0.0891  | 0.6589  |
| 3    | C      | 1.2270  | 0.0893  | 0.6582  |
| 4    | C      | 1.9233  | 0.1615  | 1.0424  |
| 5    | C      | 1.9243  | 0.1616  | 1.0430  |
| 6    | H      | -1.2191 | 0.1025  | -0.5583 |
| 7    | H      | -1.2191 | 0.1025  | -0.5583 |
| 8    | H      | -0.7668 | 0.1048  | -0.3310 |
| 9    | H      | -0.7667 | 0.1048  | -0.3310 |

Based on these results, the most reactive site for both electrophilic and nucleophilic attack is atom 5 (a carbon atom), with the highest f+ value of 1.9243 and the highest f- value of 0.1616.

No warnings were reported by the calculation engine.
